events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Scenario: crew proposes shortcut for conductor selection & sizing, submittal omits product listing page and project note that would decide whether shortcut allowed. Reference NEC 310, Table 310.16. Need evaluate field condition. Since in-progress, missing info to allow shortcut. Decision likely needs_more_info or fail? The shortcut may be non-compliant if not supported by listing/project note. But we don't have evidence it's dangerous; just missing documentation. The task says choose practical compliant course given field constraints. If work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Is proposed shortcut automatically fail? The scenario says submittal omits product listing page and project note that would decide whether shortcut is allowed. Without those, cannot verify compliance. A competent tradesperson/inspector would stop and require documentation; not accept shortcut. Decision could be needs_more_info because missing info. But if the shortcut itself is not allowed per NEC (conductor sizing must use ampacity tables, temperature corrections, etc.), maybe fail. Need parse wording: "The crew proposes a shortcut for Conductor selection & sizing, but the submittal omits the product listing page and project note that would decide whether the shortcut is allowed here." So shortcut might be allowed only if certain product listing/project note supports it. Since omitted, cannot approve. The appropriate decision: needs_more_info, risk medium, actions: request missing submittal pages/listing and project note, verify against NEC 310.16 before proceeding. component_conditions none. percent_complete maybe 40? value not asked. sound_source etc default empty? Schema doesn't say optional; must include fields. Use empty string/0/empty list. confidence 0.8. event_time 0, rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: missing documentation to justify conductor sizing shortcut. references: NEC 310, Table 310.16, 110.3(B) maybe. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

    "Crew proposes a conductor selection/sizing shortcut without the product listing page or project note needed to justify it.",
    "Submittal package is incomplete, so compliance with NEC ampacity and listing requirements cannot be verified."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt the shortcut until the missing product listing page and project note are provided.",
    "Verify the proposed conductor size and conditions of use against NEC 310 and Table 310.16.",
    "Confirm any alternative sizing method is permitted by the listing and approved submittal before installation continues."
  ],
  "rationale": "Conductor selection and sizing must be based on verified ampacity, installation conditions, and applicable product listings. The omitted listing page and project note are the evidence that would allow the shortcut; without them the crew cannot demonstrate compliance, so work must pause for documentation rather than proceed on an unverified assumption.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 310",
    "NEC Table 310.16",
    "NEC 110.3(B)"
  ]
}
